section.title: 6.1 Query Expansion
section.p: The system expands the input query using a rule-based synonym map.
section.pre: "metadata pipeline" → "metadata schema frontmatter pipeline workflow automation"
section.p: The expanded query is used for embedding generation.

section.title: 6.2 Query Intent Classification
section.p: The system performs lightweight, rule-based query intent classification prior to retrieval scoring.

li.p: identity — questions about the author (e.g., "Who is Michael Taylor?")
experience — questions about career history and projects
technical — questions about system architecture, pipelines, and implementation

section.p: Intent classification is based on keyword matching within the query.
Intent is used to dynamically adjust retrieval scoring, improving alignment between query intent and selected content.

li.p: prioritization of profile content for identity queries
stronger weighting of experience-related documents for career queries
emphasis on technical documentation for system-related questions

section.title: 6.5 Hybrid Scoring
section.p: Each candidate chunk is scored using:
Scoring is performed using a weighted hybrid model combining semantic, lexical, structural, and metadata signals.
Weights may be dynamically adjusted based on query intent.

section.title: 6.6 MMR Diversity Selection
section.p: Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) is applied to reduce redundancy and improve diversity.

ul.li: Output: reranked candidates by semantic relevance

section.title: 6.9 Knowledge Graph Expansion
section.p: A document-level graph is used to identify related documents.
Additional chunks from related documents may be appended to the result set.
